name: Franz Jackson
abstract: Franz Jackson (November 1, 1912 – May 6, 2008) was an American saxophonist and clarinetist of the Chicago jazz school.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Goin' to Chicago

works.t: No, No, Nanette: I Want to Be Happy

about_quotes.speaker: Charles "Creole Charlie" Levy
about_quotes.evidence: We had the rhythm section, myself on banjo, clientele was on piano, and Franz Jackson's drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Well, I was recommended by Franz Jackson. They were looking for a bass player for a New Year's Eve gig, and they wanted a tuba player, and they didn't know any other tuba players, so they wanted to get Franz Jackson's tuba player, and Franz Jackson said, 'I don't lend my tuba player out to anybody.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: There were two other orchestras auditioning that night and one of them was under the name of Franz Jackson. He had pulled a dirty trick on me by accepting to play with me until he found out where and for whom the audition would be, and then form his own group and undermine me for the job.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Franz Jackson was in the band... He arranged. They had 'Keep Your Shoes On, Baby' and he had a couple of arrangements in the book.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Franz Jackson (Inaudible) on the tenor saxophone. ... He's got a great Dixieland Band I understand now in Chicago.

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Barbarin
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] It's Franz Jackson's band, he's a clarinet player. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, is that right?
